title: Range Sum Query - Immutable
description: Design an algorithm that accepts an integer array and efficiently answers multiple range sum queries [left, right], returning the sum of elements between left and right inclusive.
hints: Precompute a prefix sum array during initialization.
sumRange(left, right) = prefix[right+1] - prefix[left].

title: Find Pivot Index
description: Given an array nums, return the leftmost index such that the sum of all elements to the left equals the sum of all elements to the right. If no such index exists, return -1.
hints: Compute the total sum first.
For each index, check if leftSum == totalSum - leftSum - nums[i].

title: Running Sum of 1d Array
description: Given an array nums, return the running sum where runningSum[i] = sum(nums[0]...nums[i]).
hints: You can solve this by computing a prefix sum array.
Store the cumulative sum at each index.

title: Find the Highest Altitude
description: A biker goes on a road trip with n+1 points at different altitudes. Given an array gain where gain[i] is the net gain in altitude between points i and i+1, return the highest altitude reached.
hints: Start at altitude 0 and accumulate the gains.
Track the maximum altitude seen during the traversal.

solutions.python:
def largestAltitude(gain):
    altitude = 0
    highest = 0
    for g in gain:
        altitude += g
        highest = max(highest, altitude)
    return highest

title: Product of Array Except Self
description: Given an integer array nums, return an array answer such that answer[i] is the product of all elements of nums except nums[i]. The product of any prefix or suffix of nums is guaranteed to fit in a 32-bit integer. You must solve it without using division and in O(n) time.
hints: Build a prefix product array and a suffix product array.
The answer at index i is prefix[i] * suffix[i].

solutions.python:
def productExceptSelf(nums):
    n = len(nums)
    answer = [1] * n
    left = 1
    for i in range(n):
        answer[i] = left
        left *= nums[i]
    right = 1
    for i in range(n - 1, -1, -1):
        answer[i] *= right
        right *= nums[i]
    return answer

title: Path Sum III
description: Given the root of a binary tree and an integer targetSum, return the number of paths where the sum of values along the path equals targetSum. The path does not need to start or end at the root or a leaf, but it must go downwards.
hints: Use a DFS traversal with a prefix sum hashmap.
At each node, check if (currentSum - targetSum) has been seen.

title: Continuous Subarray Sum
description: Given an integer array nums and an integer k, return true if nums has a good subarray. A good subarray has a length of at least 2 and the sum of its elements is a multiple of k.
hints: Use prefix sum modulo k.
If the same remainder appears at two indices with distance >= 2, return true.

title: Contiguous Array
description: Given a binary array nums, return the maximum length of a contiguous subarray with an equal number of 0 and 1.
hints: Treat 0 as -1 and compute a running sum.
If the same sum is seen at two indices, the subarray between them has equal 0s and 1s.

title: Subarray Sum Equals K
description: Given an array of integers nums and an integer k, return the total number of subarrays whose sum equals to k.
hints: Use a prefix sum with a hashmap to count occurrences.
For each prefix sum, check if (prefix - k) has been seen before.

title: Minimum Operations to Reduce X to Zero
description: You are given an integer array nums and an integer x. In one operation, you can remove either the leftmost or rightmost element from nums and subtract its value from x. Return the minimum number of operations to reduce x to exactly 0. If it is not possible, return -1.
hints: This is equivalent to finding the longest subarray with sum equal to totalSum - x.
Use a prefix sum hashmap to find the longest subarray.
